info.name: Marble Markets
info.attribution:
Layout, components, cards, design, and coding by Jonathan Cox (email: Contact@GettingGames.com for inquiries) and released to the Public Domain under CC0.

The sound while changing turns is "Wrong Answer" by UNIVERSFIELD and is available under the Pixabay License at https://pixabay.com/sound-effects/wrong-answer-129254/.
info.description: Place marbles to modify stock values, pay out dividends, or inflict penalties.
info.helpText:
Players take seats. Press the Setup button to begin. A random player (indicated by a button next to the seat area) is chosen to start.

The bag icon in the bottom-left shows how many discs are left in the bag. You cannot drag discs out of this area, it is only a counter.

Left-click on money numbers to change their value. Click the "End Turn" button between all turns, this will automatically deal hidden discs to the player who just ended their turn. 

Clicking the "Reveal Endgame Money Totals" button will reveal the automatically generated final scores once the game is over.

For more information, hover over the "Hover for Game Information" box in the bottom-left.

fields.label: If the game is over, then check this box and click the green button to reveal all player scores.

phase1.detail:
On Your Turn
Place all three of your discs onto the board. Each disc must be adjacent to a previously placed disc of the same color, or that color's starting hex. Activate any effect the disc covers:
-> Increase the stock matching the color that covered this icon by 1 on the track.
-> Decrease the stock matching the color that covered this icon by 1 on the track.
-> All players gain $2 for each stock of the color that covered this icon.
-> All players lose $2 for each stock of the color that covered this icon. If they don't have enough money, they must sell stocks at half (rounded down) value until they have enough to cover this penalty.

In addition to the mandatory placement of discs, up to two optional Buy/Sell actions may be performed. These can be before and/or after placing discs, but not during disc placement. To use this optional action to Buy, pay the value of a stock with at least 1 stock in the supply and take that stock into your portfolio. To use the action to sell instead, gain money equal to the value of one of your stocks and then return that stock to the supply.
Endgame
The game ends after any player turn where both conditions are met:
• 0 or 1 black background icon ($$ or //) are visible on the board.
• The +5 icon has been covered.

The game also ends in the rare case where there are not enough tokens left in the bag to deal 3 out to a player. The current number of discs in the bag is shown in the bottom-left.

Player scores are the sum of their held money and the stock value of all stocks in their portfolio. This is automatically calculated and displayed by clicking the 'Reveal Endgame Money Totals' button below this. Most money wins!

thenRoutine.note: This stops an update print when resetting a SET counter

phase1.detail:
: If this icon is showing in the top right, that means at least one other player is modifying a value (not necessarily the same as you) at the same time. This does not affect your value changes, but a notice won't be added to the history until all players have closed the modify value window. Click on the icon to forcibly close the modify value window for all players except for yourself.

Update button: Closes this window and updates your values according to the input change. This change will not show up in the history box until all players have closed the modify value window.

Change by: You may type a positive or negative value here, and this difference will automatically apply to the 'Set to' value next to it.

Set Total to: You may type any value here to indicate the total value, and this will automatically calculate the difference and show this as the 'Change by' value next to it.

+/- buttons: Click these buttons to add or subtract 1/5 value from the 'Set Total to' & 'Change by' values.
